description:
You are a capacity-planning consultant who has just received a raw resource-utilisation log that was exported from an on-premise container host.  
Your job is to transform that raw data into a concise planning report that highlights average usage and points out any “high-resource” containers.

Prerequisites already present on the system
1. Directory: /home/user/logs  
2. File: /home/user/logs/container_stats_2023-09-15.log

   • Text TSV (comma-separated) with one header line followed by one line per running container.  
   • The five columns are strictly:  
     container_id,name,cpu_percent,mem_usage_mb  
   • Example of the first two data lines (the actual file contains six lines of data):  
     1db3aa112,web_frontend,35.5,512  
     2ab4cc223,database,82.7,1500  

Task you must complete
A. Parse every line of /home/user/logs/container_stats_*.log (the filename pattern is important—there might be more logs on future days).  
B. Derive the following metrics, using **every** data line in the file you are given:  
   1. Total number of containers.  
   2. Average CPU utilisation (percentage) across all containers, rounded to two decimal places.  
   3. Average memory usage (MiB) across all containers, rounded to one decimal place.  
C. Flag any container that meets **either** of these thresholds:  
   • cpu_percent > 75.0 **OR** mem_usage_mb > 1024.  
D. Produce exactly one plain-text report file at /home/user/reports/capacity_report.txt with the following fixed layout (line breaks are UNIX “\n”; do not add extra blank lines):  

Capacity Planning Report  
Date: YYYY-MM-DD            ← must match the date embedded in the source log’s filename  

Summary:  
Total Containers: <integer>  
Average CPU (%): <two-decimal-number>  
Average Memory (MB): <one-decimal-number>  

High Resource Containers:  
<container_id>,<name>,CPU=<cpu_percent>,MEM=<mem_usage_mb>  
…one line per high-resource container in the same order they appeared in the source log; if none, write exactly the word “None” on that line.

E. Create the parent directory /home/user/reports/ if it does not already exist.  
F. Do not alter or delete the original log file.

Hints (not commands):
• You can use standard CLI tools (awk, cut, paste, bc, etc.) or a quick script in the language of your choice that is likely pre-installed (Python 3 is available).  
• Make sure floating-point formatting exactly matches the required decimal precision.  
• Remember that the header line is not data.  
• After you finish, you can cat the report to verify the formatting visually.

The automated test will:
1. Verify that /home/user/reports/capacity_report.txt exists.  
2. Check that every line is present exactly as specified above—including the date, the computed averages, and the high-resource container list—in the correct order with correct whitespace.  
3. Ensure that the original log remains unchanged.

Good luck!
truth:
Pre-existing directory and file:
1. /home/user/logs/
2. /home/user/logs/container_stats_2023-09-15.log  
   Exact contents (with trailing newline on every line):

container_id,name,cpu_percent,mem_usage_mb
1db3aa112,web_frontend,35.5,512
2ab4cc223,database,82.7,1500
3cd5dd334,cache,18.2,256
4de6ee445,worker,77.9,1100
5ef7ff556,nginx_proxy,23.0,450
6fa8gg667,analytics,69.4,2048

Expected file the agent must create:
/home/user/reports/capacity_report.txt  
Exact required contents (each line terminated by “\n” and **no extra blank lines at the end**):

Capacity Planning Report
Date: 2023-09-15

Summary:
Total Containers: 6
Average CPU (%): 51.12
Average Memory (MB): 977.7

High Resource Containers:
2ab4cc223,database,CPU=82.7,MEM=1500
4de6ee445,worker,CPU=77.9,MEM=1100
6fa8gg667,analytics,CPU=69.4,MEM=2048

(The directory /home/user/reports/ does not exist before the task begins; its creation is part of the task.)
